author: tuyen4818
content:
lodestar also queues them + trigger block search and a reprocess when block is imported
we do that for a lot of messages too so the cache could be huge (especially for beacon_attestation)

author: potuz
content:
Ah I posted elsewhere. The bug we had happened when three things happened at the same time: 

1. We got columns before the block (we queue them)
2. The block was later orphaned 
3. We kept trying to request those columns and no one gave them so we downscored them until isolation

author: qu0b
content: <@993802539856314398> it seems all teku nodes have forked off

author: qu0b
content:
>   1. 05:40:36 UTC — teku-nimbusel-1's nimbus-el wrongly rejected the canonical, geth-built, now-finalized exec block 61738 0xe4af524c… with wrong receiptRoot (computed 0x0f4eb56e… vs header 0xc13ad4e3…).
>   This is the known regression: a top-frame-prep-failed tx after a successful tx gets receipt status=1, corrupting the receipts root nimbus-el computes.
>   2. Teku treats the EL's INVALID verdict as authoritative, so it marked the entire canonical branch invalid — its finality froze at epoch 2188 (network is at 2253) and it can never rejoin while running the
>   buggy build (deterministic re-rejection).
>   3. Teku then kept extending the last branch its EL would accept — a private fork. ~50 epochs past the divergence, the RANDAO/proposer shuffle on that fork differs completely, so teku's own validators keep
>   landing proposer slots there. At slot 71746 it proposed fork block 0x4c64408f… (proposer 2246); the real network's block for that slot came from nimbus-ethrex-1 (proposer 1404) and is canonical. Dora
>   indexes teku's fork block as "Missed (Orphaned)". Its fork head was at 72182 when I checked — these orphan rows will keep appearing every time one of its validators is shuffled in.
